Off Calendar Jan-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Martin Zurada, Esq. 925-937-3900 of Venardi Zurada for Plaintiff.
Victor Fu, Esq. 424-239-1908 for Defendant through the Court's telephone.

RULING - A R G U E D. The matter was passed to have Defendant's Counsel present on the phone.
The matter is recalled at 10:23 am. Plaintiff argues and clarifies the scops of the stay. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT INSTQUEST INC. DBA VERLOCAL'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Dismiss Or, Alternatively, Stay The Action.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Instquest Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings. The FAA applies to this case because it involves interstate commerce as acknowledged in the complaint. Instquest is a Delaware corporation and its on-line services implicate interstate commerce. Paragraph 13 of the parties' agreement expressly states that the FAA will govern with the limited exception that the procedural rules of the CAA apply. The FAA preempts application of Labor Code 229, which allows an employee to litigate wage claims notwithstanding an arbitration agreement. (Hoover v. American Income Life Ins. Co. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 1193, 1207.) The agreement has an enforceable delegation clause, so the arbitrator will decide the scope and proper parties to the arbitration proceeding. Paragraph 13B provides that the JAMS employment rules apply. JAMS employment Rule 11(b), which was promulgated on July 1, 2014, provides that the arbitrator decides all jurisdictional and arbitrability disputes. The parties entered into their agreement on July 27, 2014 and they clearly and unmistakably delegated the issue of arbitrability to the arbitrator by referencing these rules in their agreement. (Dream Theater, Inc. v. Dream Theater (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 547, 557.) (as fully reported on the reporter's transcript)

Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.
